extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters initiated by versus against the relevant parties: I counted only the “Against” rows—criminal proceedings of 3 against the Company plus nil against Directors, Promoters and Subsidiary; statutory/regulatory actions are nil across all against rows; and tax proceedings are 3 against the Company plus nil against Directors, Promoters and Subsidiary. For the aggregate amount against the Company and Subsidiary, I used the quantified “Against our Company” amount of ₹67.25 million and excluded all “By our Company” and personal Director matters; this converts to ₹6.725 crore. The contingent-liabilities figure is the stated consolidated Total of ₹3,312.84 million as of September 30, 2023, not a re-sum of line items, converting to ₹331.284 crore. The 62.7% and 63.4% concentration figures relate to AUM in three states, not company-wide top-customer revenue; therefore customer concentration percentages are not disclosed. Monetary tables use ₹ million, converted to crore by dividing by 10 (₹1 million = ₹0.1 crore).
